channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Negotiators get closer to agreeing on a plastic pollution treaty
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — Negotiators working on a treaty to address the global crisis of plastic pollution inched closer to an agreement Friday, with more countries saying they want to address the total plastic on Earth.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syria insurgents push their advance toward 2nd largest city Aleppo
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of Syrian insurgents pushed on with their advances on government-held areas in the country’s northwest, reaching the outskirts of Syria’s second largest city Aleppo and wrestling control of several strategic towns and villages along the way, activists and fighters said Friday
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ireland votes in a close-run election where incumbents hope to cling on to power
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — Ireland is voting Friday in a parliamentary election that will decide the next government — and will show whether Ireland bucks the global trend of incumbents being ousted by disgruntled voters after years of pandemic, international instability and a cost-of-living pressures.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral unveils its new interior 5 years after devastating fire
item.description: After more than five years of frenetic reconstruction work, Notre Dame Cathedral has showed its new self to the world Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Missing Canadian hiker found alive after spending weeks in blistering cold
item.description: A missing hiker was located in the Canadian wilderness after being lost for more than six weeks, according to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Georgian protesters and police clash after prime minister suspends talks on joining the EU
item.description: Police and protesters have clashed in Georgia after the country’s ruling party suspended negotiations to join the European Union until 2028.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Chad ends a defense cooperation agreement with France, its former colonial ruler
item.description: Chad’s government says it is ending a defense cooperation agreement with France, its former colonial ruler, to redefine the nation's sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukrainian energy workers carry out repairs despite Russia's pounding of the country's power grid
item.description: Workers at one of Ukraine's thermal power plants struggle to repair damage and keep electricity flowing as Russia continues its campaign of attacks targeting the country's power grid.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Embattled Japan leader vows to cooperate with long-ignored opposition in bid to save his government
item.description: Japan’s embattled Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, who survived as leader after a major election loss by his governing coalition, is promising to work closely with the long-ignored opposition that is now the only way his minority government can stay in power.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian defense minister visits North Korea for talks with military and political leaders
item.description: Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov is in North Korea for talks with its military and political leaders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Battered by war and divisions, Lebanon faces a long list of challenges after ceasefire deal
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hours after a U.S.-brokered ceasefire to end the war between Israel and Hezbollah went into effect, Lebanon woke up to the sound of celebratory gunfire instead of Israeli airstrikes and drones buzzing overhead.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia suspends talks on joining the European Union and accuses the bloc of blackmail
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Georgia is suspending talks on its bid to join the European Union for four years, its prime minister said Thursday, in view of what he described as “blackmail and manipulation” from some of the bloc's politicians.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: High-ranking military official in China suspended and placed under investigation
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A member of China's powerful Central Military Commission has been suspended and put under investigation, the country's Defense Ministry said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DAKE KANG and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Displaced families in Lebanon who return home are faced with piles of rubble
item.description: HANOUIYEH, Lebanon (AP) — During their 37 years of marriage, Mariam Kourani and her husband ran a butcher shop in southern Lebanon, started a business selling serving containers and opened a small restaurant. An Israeli airstrike in late September destroyed it all.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Avocados, tequila and other iconic Mexican products are jeopardized by Trump's tariff threats
item.description: URUAPAN, Mexico (AP) — Mexicans are worried that threats by Donald Trump to impose 25% tariffs could affect a wide range of iconic Mexican products and threaten entire regional economies. In western Mexico, no crop supplies an income for so many small growers as avocados.
item.dc:creator: ARMANDO SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sweden asks for China's cooperation over Baltic Sea cables cut while a Chinese ship was nearby
item.description: HARPSUND, Sweden (AP) — Sweden has formally asked China to cooperate in explaining the recent rupture of two data cables on the Baltic Sea bed in an area where a China-flagged vessel had been sighted, Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel, Hezbollah accuse each other of violating ceasefire
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah accused each other of violating terms in the temporary ceasefire that went into effect Wednesday, according to multiple reports. The accusations began after Israel fired on southern Lebanon on Wednesday, wounding two people.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Syrian insurgents launch large-scale attack on government forces in the country's northwest
item.description: Syrian armed groups launched a large-scale attack on areas controlled by government forces and seized territory in northwestern Syria, opposition groups said Thursday. The government and its allies responded with airstrikes and shelling to ward off the insurgent advances, activists said.
item.dc:creator: BEIRUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian strike on Ukrainian energy grid leaves more than 1M without power
item.description: A Russian strike on energy infrastructure in western Ukraine on Thursday left more than 1 million people without power, according to local officials. Electricity was knocked out for more than 500,000 households in the Lviv region due to the attack, according to regional Gov. Maksym Kozytskyi.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Biden admin urges Ukraine to lower draft age to conscript 18-year-olds
item.description: The Biden administration is urging Ukraine to lower its draft age to conscript 18-year-olds to keep pace with Russia’s growing military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Space Force looks to bulk up against anti-satellite weapons
item.description: The 5-year-old U.S. Space Force is moving quickly to confront what is becoming its priority challenge: the threat of anti-satellite weapons (ASATs) from foreign adversaries, including Russia and China.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Thousands of Lebanese return to their homes as Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire takes hold
item.description: TYRE, Lebanon (AP) — Thousands of Lebanese displaced by the war between Israel and Hezbollah militants returned home Wednesday as a ceasefire took hold, driving cars stacked with personal belongings and defying warnings from Lebanese and Israeli troops to avoid some areas.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ruling may further delay count of Puerto Rico's still-uncertified votes in Nov. 5 elections
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The U.S. territory of Puerto Rico has yet to produce a certified vote tally for a single district almost a month after holding historic general elections.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: US tells Ukraine to 'look hard' at lowering draft age in Russia war
item.description: The United States is urging Ukraine to “look hard” at removing policy mandates that limit the nation to drafting soldiers aged 25 and up, a senior administration official told The Hill on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: State Department downgrades travel advisory for China
item.description: The State Department on Wednesday downgraded its travel advisory for China, shortly after the White House announced it had secured the release of three Americans as part of a prisoner swap with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: 5 key takeaways from Israel-Lebanon ceasefire brokered by US
item.description: President Biden’s announcement Tuesday of a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon marked a significant foreign policy achievement weeks before he leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Democrat calls for sanctions over violent clashes in Pakistan
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna (D-Calif.) denounced Pakistan’s chief of army staff Wednesday for allegedly killing citizens and confiscating death records from medics in an effort to subdue growing protests in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump taps Keith Kellogg as special envoy to Ukraine and Russia
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced his appointment of Keith Kellogg, a retired lieutenant general who served as chief of staff on Trump’s National Security Council during his first term, as special envoy to Ukraine and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: US sanctions 21 more Maduro allies accused of post-election repression in Venezuela
item.description: The U.S. has imposed sanctions on 21 more allies of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro for repressing Venezuelans following the country's disputed presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian police officer who used Taser on 95-year-old woman found guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A police officer who shocked a 95-year-old nursing home resident with a Taser was found guilty of manslaughter in an Australian court, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
